    Structural correctness of the zsubst table (mirror iR2/iotil) #

    The table read-out + diagonal unfolding + per-constructor recursion equations, proven exactly as the iR2/iotil analogs in InternalZ.lean. The payoff is fstIdx_zsubst and the recursion equations that ZDerivation_zsubst (rung-1 correctness) will consume.

    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubstTable_seq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t n : V) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (zsubstTable a t n)
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubstTable_lh {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t n : V) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zsubstTable a t n) = n + 1
    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.znth_zsubstTable_succ {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t : V) {n k : V} (hk : k < n + 1) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zsubstTable a t (n + 1)) k = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zsubstTable a t n) k
    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.znth_zsubstTable_eq_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t N k : V) :
    k ≤ N → LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zsubstTable a t N) k = zsubst k a t
    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_eq_zsubstNext {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t : V) {c : V} (hpos : 0 < c) :
    zsubst c a t = zsubstNext c (zsubstTable a t (c - 1)) a t

    zsubst recursion equations (per Z-rule) #

    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zAtom {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s a t : V) :
    zsubst (zAtom s) a t = zAtom (fvSubstSeqt a t s)
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zIall {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s e p d0 a t : V) :
    zsubst (zIall s e p d0) a t = zIall (fvSubstSeqt a t s) e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p) (zsubst d0 a t)
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zIneg {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s p d0 a t : V) :
    zsubst (zIneg s p d0) a t = zIneg (fvSubstSeqt a t s) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p) (zsubst d0 a t)
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zInd {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s e u p d0 d1 a t : V) :
    zsubst (zInd s ⟪e, u⟫ p d0 d1) a t = zInd (fvSubstSeqt a t s) ⟪e, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t u⟫ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p) (zsubst d0 a t) (zsubst d1 a t)
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s r ds a t : V) :
    zsubst (zK s r ds) a t = zK (fvSubstSeqt a t s) r (tblMapSeq (zsubstTable a t (zK s r ds - 1)) ds)
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zAxAll {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s p k a t : V) :
    zsubst (zAxAll s p k) a t = zAxAll (fvSubstSeqt a t s) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p) k
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zAxNeg {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s p a t : V) :
    zsubst (zAxNeg s p) a t = zAxNeg (fvSubstSeqt a t s) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p)
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zAx1 {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s C a t : V) :
    zsubst (zAx1 s C) a t = zAx1 (fvSubstSeqt a t s) C
    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zsubst_zAxBot {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s a t : V) :
    zsubst (zAxBot s) a t = zAxBot (fvSubstSeqt a t s)

    fstIdx_zsubst — the end-sequent of the substituted derivation computes (rung-1 step 1) #

    For any genuine Z-derivation d, the reduct's end-sequent is the substituted end-sequent. Proven by the 7-way ZDerivation case split (each constructor's recursion equation + fstIdx (z* s' …) = s').

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fstIdx_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (a t : V) (hZ : ZDerivation d) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (zsubst d a t) = fvSubstSeqt a t (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d)

    Substitution-commutation substrate for ZDerivation_zsubst (rung-1 step 2) #

    The per-Z-rule transfer lemmas the genuine correctness ZDerivation_zsubst will consume:

    • inAnt_fvSubstSeq — antecedent membership is preserved (atom + Ax cases; no freshness needed).
    • fvSubst_inegF — fvSubst commutes with inegF (the zIneg/zAxNeg succedent).
    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.inAnt_fvSubstSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t A Γ : V} (h : inAnt A Γ) :
    inAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t A) (fvSubstSeq a t Γ)

    Antecedent membership transfers under fvSubstSeq. If A ∈ Γ (positionally) then fvSubst a t A ∈ fvSubstSeq a t Γ — the atom-rule and ∀/¬-axiom cases of ZDerivation_zsubst.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubstSeq_seqCons {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t Γ A : V} (hΓ : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq Γ) :
    fvSubstSeq a t (Γ ⁀' A) = fvSubstSeq a t Γ ⁀' LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t A

    fvSubstSeq commutes with seqCons: mapping fvSubst a t over Γ ⁀' A equals consing fvSubst a t A onto the mapped Γ (needs Seq Γ, since fvSubstSeq/seqCons are positional). The genuine I¬-rule premise-antecedent shape seqAnt (fstIdx d0) = seqCons (seqAnt s) p (= the would-be zInegAntWff conjunct) transfers through ZDerivation_zsubst via this — modulo a Seq (seqAnt s) invariant that the current ZPhi skeleton does NOT yet record (see PENDING_WORK lap 131).

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubst_inegF {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t p : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ t) (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ p) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (inegF p) = inegF (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p)

    fvSubst commutes with inegF (inegF p = ∼p ⋎ ⊥), via fvSubst_neg. Needed to transfer the zIneg conclusion succedent inegF p under eigenvariable substitution.

    Term-substitution helpers for the zInd succedent terms (rung-1 step A) #

    The zInd rule's three succedent terms — numeral 0, Sa = ^&e ^+ numeral 1 (e the eigenvariable, e ≠ a), and the conclusion term zIndTerm d — must be transferred through termFvSubst a t. The numeral/Sa cases are FIXED by e ≠ a-freshness (they contain no ^&a); only zIndTerm d is genuinely renamed (its closedness is supplied by the zIndWff conjunct).

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.termFvSubst_qqAdd {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t x y : V) (hx : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ x) (hy : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ y) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (x ^+ y) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t x ^+ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t y

    termFvSubst commutes with qqAdd (binary + function node). termFvSubst_func carries hypotheses so it does not auto-fire in a bare simp; we discharge IsFunc 2 addIndex / IsUTermVec 2 ?[x,y] explicitly.

    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.termFvSubst_numeral {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t x : V) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral x) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral x

    termFvSubst fixes any numeral (numerals contain no free variables). Mirrors numeral_substs.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.termFvSubst_succVar {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t e : V} (he : e ≠ a) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFvar e ^+ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 1) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFvar e ^+ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 1

    The zInd minor-premise succedent term Sa = ^&e ^+ numeral 1 is fixed by termFvSubst a t provided the eigenvariable e ≠ a (Buchholz regularity).

    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.isSemiterm_succVar {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (e : V) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsSemiterm ℒₒᵣ 0 (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFvar e ^+ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 1)

    Sa = ^&e ^+ numeral 1 is a closed semiterm.

    Free-variable non-occurrence: transferring fvSubst a (numeral m) p = p across the target #

    The cut-elimination ∀-inversion (ZDerivation_iRcritG_critReductCorr, Crux2Blueprint) needs the eigenvariable-condition facts fvSubst a (numeral k') p = p and fvSubstSeq a (numeral k') Γ = Γ at the critical I∀ node, for the cut instance k' — which is not known at the I∀ node. The storable, 𝚫₁, red/zsubst-stable witness of "a does not occur free in p" is the single equation fvSubst a (numeral 0) p = p (substituting any a-free closed term — here numeral 0 — fixes p iff ^&a ∉ FV p). These lemmas transfer it to any numeral target, via the double-substitution-collapses identity: a numeral contains no ^&a, so after ^&a ↦ numeral m no ^&a remains, and re-substituting ^&a ↦ s is a no-op. This is the substrate that lets a standalone freshness invariant (à la zReg, lap-93 additive O1) supply the inversion's hpfresh/hΓfresh — without baking freshness into zIallWff/ZPhi (which would shrink the ZDerivation fixpoint and force the embedding to re-prove it, lap-93 architecture note above) and without a code bound (p ≤ a is not zsubst-stable, lap-92).

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.termFvSubst_numeral_idem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a s m u : V} (hu : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ u) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a s (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) u) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) u

    Term-level collapse. Substituting ^&a ↦ s after ^&a ↦ numeral m is a no-op: the inner substitution already replaced every ^&a by the a-free numeral m.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.termFvSubstVec_numeral_idem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a s m k v : V} (hv : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTermVec ℒₒᵣ k v) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubstVec ℒₒᵣ a s k (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubstVec ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) k v) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubstVec ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) k v

    Term-vector collapse (the rel/nrel ingredient of the formula collapse).

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubst_numeral_idem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a s m p : V} (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ p) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a s (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) p) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) p

    Formula-level collapse. fvSubst a s (fvSubst a (numeral m) p) = fvSubst a (numeral m) p.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubst_numeral_transfer {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a m k p : V} (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ p) (h : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) p = p) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral k) p = p

    Freshness transfer (formula). From the storable witness fvSubst a (numeral m) p = p (non-occurrence of ^&a), the substitution ^&a ↦ numeral k is the identity for any k. This is exactly the form ZDerivation_iRcritG_critReductCorr's hpfresh needs at the cut instance k.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubstSeq_numeral_idem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a s m Γ : V} (hΓ : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh Γ, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth Γ i)) :
    fvSubstSeq a s (fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) Γ) = fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) Γ

    Sequence-level collapse. fvSubstSeq a s (fvSubstSeq a (numeral m) Γ) = fvSubstSeq a (numeral m) Γ when every entry of Γ is a UFormula.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubstSeq_numeral_transfer {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a m k Γ : V} (hΓ : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh Γ, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth Γ i)) (h : fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) Γ = Γ) :
    fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral k) Γ = Γ

    Freshness transfer (sequence). The hΓfresh analogue of fvSubst_numeral_transfer for the antecedent sequence Γ = seqAnt sᵢ.

    Freshness is PRESERVED under closed-numeral substitution (the zFresh_zsubst substrate, lap 127) #

    zReg_zsubst (an equality) needs no well-formedness because maxEigen_zsubst is purely structural. zFresh is different: substituting away the eigenvariable can only make a node more fresh, so the right statement is the downward preservation ZFresh d → ZFresh (zsubst d a (numeral n)), not an equality (which fails at an I∀ node whose eigenvariable is a). The engine of that preservation is the commutation of two distinct fresh-variable numeral substitutions below: since numerals are closed, ^&a ↦ numeral n and ^&e ↦ numeral m commute whenever e ≠ a. Combined with fvSubst_numeral_idem (the e = a collapse), it gives "non-occurrence of ^&e survives substituting a different variable by a closed numeral" — exactly the per-I∀-node freshFlag-preservation step.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.termFvSubst_numeral_comm {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a e m n : V} (hea : e ≠ a) {u : V} (hu : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ u) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) u) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubst ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) u)

    Term-level commutation of two distinct fresh-variable numeral substitutions (e ≠ a).

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.termFvSubstVec_numeral_comm {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a e m n k v : V} (hea : e ≠ a) (hv : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTermVec ℒₒᵣ k v) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubstVec ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) k (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubstVec ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) k v) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubstVec ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) k (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.termFvSubstVec ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) k v)

    Term-vector commutation (the rel/nrel ingredient of the formula commutation).

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubst_numeral_comm {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a e m n : V} (hea : e ≠ a) {p : V} (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ p) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) p) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral m) p)

    Formula-level commutation (e ≠ a): the engine of downward freshness preservation.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubst_numeral_fresh_subst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a e n p : V} (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ p) (h : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) p = p) :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) p) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) p

    Downward freshness preservation (formula). If ^&e does not occur free in p (fvSubst e (numeral 0) p = p), it still does not occur after substituting a different/closed numeral target into a (possibly different) variable a — i.e. fvSubst a (numeral n) cannot introduce ^&e.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubstSeq_numeral_fresh_subst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a e n Γ : V} (hΓ : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh Γ, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth Γ i)) (h : fvSubstSeq e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) Γ = Γ) :
    fvSubstSeq e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) (fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) Γ) = fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) Γ

    Downward freshness preservation (sequence). The hΓfresh/antecedent analogue: if ^&e is free in no entry of Γ (fvSubstSeq e (numeral 0) Γ = Γ), it remains absent after fvSubstSeq a (numeral n).

    Substitution-invariants for the zK chain-validity transfer (rung-1 step C.zK groundwork) #

    zKValid subst-invariance reads the chain through irk/tp/iperm/isChainInf; the foundational fact is that fvSubst (substituting a closed term for a free variable) leaves the logical complexity irk unchanged — it only touches atomic subterms.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.irk_fvSubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ t) {A : V} :
    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ A → irk (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t A) = irk A

    irk is invariant under fvSubst (rk counts logical structure; substituting a closed term for a free variable touches only atoms). The rank ingredient of isChainInf subst-invariance.

    source
    @[simp]
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zTag_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t d : V} (hd : ZDerivation d) :
    zTag (zsubst d a t) = zTag d

    zsubst preserves the rule tag (for a genuine Z-derivation): substituting a free variable rebuilds the same Z-rule, so zTag is unchanged. Feeds the tag-gated formula-hood conjuncts of the zKValid chain-validity transfer.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iperm_tp_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsSemiterm ℒₒᵣ 0 t) {d q : V} (hd : ZDerivation d) (h : iperm (tp d) q) :
    iperm (tp (zsubst d a t)) (fvSubstSeqt a t q)

    Permissibility (iperm, Lemma 3.3) transfers under fvSubst. For a genuine Z-derivation d, if its rule symbol tp d permits a sequent q, then the substituted symbol tp (zsubst d a t) permits the substituted sequent fvSubstSeqt a t q. The principal formula (R-symbol succedent / L-symbol cut formula) and the sequent's succedent/antecedent transform CONSISTENTLY by fvSubst, so the match is preserved. This is the positive (iperm) conjunct of the zKValid chain-validity transfer; the criticality (¬iperm vs the chain conclusion s) does NOT transfer this cleanly — fvSubst can collapse a previously-distinct principal-formula/conclusion pair (e.g. ^∀F(^&a) vs ^∀F(t)), so a spurious match can appear. Closing the zK case of ZDerivation_zsubst therefore needs an eigenvariable-freshness hypothesis (a ∉ FV(s)); see PENDING_WORK.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.isChainInf_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t s r ds ds' : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ t) (hlh : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds' = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hZ : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) (hmap : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds' i = zsubst (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) a t) (hcf : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (chainAsucc ds i)) (h : isChainInf s r ds) :
    isChainInf (fvSubstSeqt a t s) r ds'

    isChainInf transfers under eigenvariable substitution (the chain-structure conjunct of zKValid). Given a chain s r ds whose premises are Z-derivations and whose succedents are genuine formulas, the substituted chain (fvSubstSeqt a t s) r ds' — where ds' lists zsubst (znth ds i) a t — is still a valid isChainInf. The point is that every condition is positive (closed under applying fvSubst), so they are preserved by the consistent substitution, NOT merely vacuously fixed: the A_{j₀}∈{C,⊥} condition by fvSubst_falsum + congruence, the antecedent threading by inAnt_fvSubstSeq, and the rank bound by irk_fvSubst (rank invariance — this is the one conjunct that consumes the succedent formula-hood hcf). This corrects the lap-76 worry: the chain structure transfers cleanly; only the zKValid criticality conjunct (a negative ¬iperm) is delicate.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.inAnt_fvSubstSeq_reflect {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t A Γ : V} (hΓ : Γ ≤ a) (hfs : ∀ k < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh Γ, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth Γ k)) (h : inAnt A (fvSubstSeq a t Γ)) :
    inAnt A Γ

    Reflection of inAnt through fvSubstSeq on an a-free formula sequence: if A occurs in the substituted antecedent fvSubstSeq a t Γ and every entry of Γ ≤ a is a genuine formula, then A already occurs in Γ. The reverse of inAnt_fvSubstSeq — its entries are a-free so fvSubst fixes them. The load-bearing step of the zK-criticality transfer's L-symbol case.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.tp_zsubst_eq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsSemiterm ℒₒᵣ 0 t) {d : V} (hd : ZDerivation d) (hda : d ≤ a) :
    tp (zsubst d a t) = tp d

    tp is invariant under eigenvariable substitution on an a-free derivation (d ≤ a): the principal formula is ≤ a hence ^&a-free, so fvSubst fixes it and the inference symbol is unchanged.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iperm_zsubst_conclusion {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t s I : V} (hsa : s ≤ a) (hssf : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (seqSucc s)) (hsaf : ∀ k < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (seqAnt s), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (seqAnt s) k)) (h : iperm I (fvSubstSeqt a t s)) :
    iperm I s

    Permissibility against an a-free well-formed conclusion reflects through substitution. If the substituted symbol I permits the substituted conclusion fvSubstSeqt a t s and s ≤ a is a genuine sequent (succedent + antecedent formulas), then I already permits s. The conclusion is ^&a-free so its succedent/antecedent are fixed by fvSubst; the L-symbol case uses inAnt_fvSubstSeq_reflect. This turns the zKValid criticality ¬iperm (tp dᵢ) s into ¬iperm (tp (zsubst dᵢ)) (fvSubstSeqt s).

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zIallF_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t d : V} (hd : ZDerivation d) (h : zTag d = 1) :
    zIallF (zsubst d a t) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (zIallF d)

    Principal-formula read-out under substitution (tag 1): zIallF commutes with zsubst.

    source
    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zInegF_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a t d : V} (hd : ZDerivation d) (h : zTag d = 2) :
    zInegF (zsubst d a t) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (zInegF d)

    Principal-formula read-out under substitution (tag 2): zInegF commutes with zsubst.

    source

    zAxAllF (zsubst d a t) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (zAxAllF d)

    zAxNegF (zsubst d a t) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t (zAxNegF d)

    Principal-formula read-out under substitution (tag 6): zAxNegF commutes with zsubst.

    maxEigen — the largest eigenvariable index in a derivation (Path-X freshness foundation) #

    maxEigen d = the maximum eigenvariable index over all zIall/zInd nodes of d (0 if none). Built by the exact idg table template (InternalZ.lean): maxEigenNext d s reads the premise results out of the running table s and folds in this node's own eigenvariable. The point (lap-92 DECISION): a freshness invariant phrased on maxEigen is stable under zsubst (closed-term substitution preserves the eigenvariable binders), unlike the code bound d ≤ a — so it is maintainable through red.

    source

                    Structural correctness of the maxEigen table (mirror idg) #

                    Identical course-of-values bookkeeping to idgTable (InternalZ.lean:1920): the length-(N+1) table maxEigenTable N has every in-range entry equal to the genuine maxEigen value, so the table-reduction unfolds to maxEigen c = maxEigenNext c (maxEigenTable (c-1)) for positive c.

                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigenTable_seq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (n : V) :
                    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (maxEigenTable n)
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigenTable_lh {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (n : V) :
                    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (maxEigenTable n) = n + 1
                    source
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.znth_maxEigenTable_succ {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {n k : V} (hk : k < n + 1) :
                    LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (maxEigenTable (n + 1)) k = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (maxEigenTable n) k
                    source
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.znth_maxEigenTable_eq_maxEigen {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (N k : V) :
                    k ≤ N → LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (maxEigenTable N) k = maxEigen k

                    Table stability: every entry of the length-(N+1) table is the genuine maxEigen value.

                    source
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_eq_maxEigenNext {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {c : V} (hpos : 0 < c) :
                    maxEigen c = maxEigenNext c (maxEigenTable (c - 1))

                    maxEigen c = maxEigenNext c (maxEigenTable (c-1)) for positive codes.

                    maxEigen recursion equations (Path-X freshness foundation) #

                    The largest eigenvariable index folds structurally: each zIall/zInd node contributes its own eigenvariable; chains/negations/atoms/axioms contribute nothing of their own. These mirror the idg recursion equations one-for-one. The point (lap-92 DECISION): combined with maxEigen_zsubst (next) these make a maxEigen-phrased freshness invariant maintainable.

                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zAtom {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s : V) :
                    maxEigen (zAtom s) = 0
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zIall {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s a p d0 : V) :
                    maxEigen (zIall s a p d0) = a ⊔ maxEigen d0
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zIneg {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s p d0 : V) :
                    maxEigen (zIneg s p d0) = maxEigen d0
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zInd {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s at' p d0 d1 : V) :
                    maxEigen (zInd s at' p d0 d1) = π₁ at' ⊔ (maxEigen d0 ⊔ maxEigen d1)
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zAxAll {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s p k : V) :
                    maxEigen (zAxAll s p k) = 0
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zAxNeg {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s p : V) :
                    maxEigen (zAxNeg s p) = 0
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zAx1 {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s C : V) :
                    maxEigen (zAx1 s C) = 0
                    source
                    @[simp]
                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zAxBot {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s : V) :
                    maxEigen (zAxBot s) = 0

                    maxEigen-fold over a premise sequence (for the variadic K^r equation) #

                    iseqMaxEigen ds = max_{i < lh ds} maxEigen(znth ds i) — the genuine fold (applies maxEigen directly). The K^r step in maxEigenNext reads the table form iseqMaxTab (maxEigenTable M) ds; under dominance the two agree (mirror iseqMaxIdg/idg_zK).

                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqMaxAux_maxEigenTable_eq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {M ds : V} (hdom : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i ≤ M) (j : V) :
                              j ≤ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds → iseqMaxAux (maxEigenTable M) ds j = iseqMaxEigenAux ds j

                              Table-fold = maxEigen-fold under dominance.

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s r ds : V) (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) :
                              maxEigen (zK s r ds) = iseqMaxEigen ds

                              The variadic K^r eigenvariable equation: a chain node has no eigenvariable of its own, so maxEigen (zK s r ds) = max_j maxEigen(dⱼ).

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.le_iseqMaxEigenAux {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds : V} (j i : V) :
                              i < j → maxEigen (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) ≤ iseqMaxEigenAux ds j

                              Every premise's maxEigen is dominated by the partial fold.

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.le_iseqMaxEigen {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds i : V} (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                              maxEigen (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) ≤ iseqMaxEigen ds

                              The full fold dominates each premise's maxEigen (for i < lh ds).

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqMaxEigenAux_congr {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {A B : V} (hpt : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh A, maxEigen (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth A i) = maxEigen (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth B i)) (j : V) :
                              j ≤ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh A → iseqMaxEigenAux A j = iseqMaxEigenAux B j

                              Fold congruence: equal lengths + entrywise-equal maxEigen ⟹ equal folds (the chain step of maxEigen_zsubst).

                              maxEigen_zsubst — eigenvariable indices are stable under closed-term substitution (Path-X §2b) #

                              The substitution crux of the DECISION: zsubst d a t rewrites every node's data (sequent / formula / term) but leaves every zIall/zInd eigenvariable index untouched (cf. zsubst_zIall keeping e, zsubst_zInd keeping π₁ at'). Hence maxEigen is invariant. Proved by zDerivation_induction, the maxEigen recursion equations, and (chain case) the fold congruence above. This is what makes a maxEigen-phrased freshness invariant maintainable through red — the code bound d ≤ a was not.

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t d : V) :
                              ZDerivation d → maxEigen (zsubst d a t) = maxEigen d

                              iord_zsubst — the eigensubst preserves the ordinal assignment (route-B I∀ bridge, lap 96) #

                              The route-B faithful red must, on the I∀ rule, perform Buchholz's eigenvariable substitution red (zIall) = d0(a/n) (currently red (zIall) = d0, conclusion untracked). For the ε₀-descent to survive that rewire, the eigensubst must not change the ordinal: iord (zsubst d a t) = iord d. This holds because zsubst rewrites node data (sequents/formulae/terms) but preserves every node's TAG and RANK and maps premises recursively (zsubst_zK keeps r; zsubst_zIall/_zInd keep the eigenvariable), and iord = iotower (iotil d) (idg d) reads only tags/ranks/premise-ordinals. Proved by the same zDerivation_induction + fold-congruence template as maxEigen_zsubst.

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqMaxIdgAux_congr_val {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {A B : V} (hpt : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh A, idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth A i) = idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth B i)) (j : V) :
                              j ≤ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh A → iseqMaxIdgAux A j = iseqMaxIdgAux B j

                              idg-fold value-congruence: entrywise-equal idg ⟹ equal partial folds (the chain step of idg_zsubst; the existing iseqMaxIdgAux_congr requires znth-equality, too strong here since zsubst changes the premises but preserves their idg). Mirror of iseqMaxEigenAux_congr.

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqNaddIdgAux_congr_val {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {A B : V} (hpt : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh A, iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth A i) = iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth B i)) (j : V) :
                              j ≤ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh A → iseqNaddIdgAux A j = iseqNaddIdgAux B j

                              iõ-fold value-congruence: entrywise-equal iotil ⟹ equal partial folds (the chain step of iotil_zsubst). Mirror of iseqMaxIdgAux_congr_val.

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.idg_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ t) (a d : V) :
                              ZDerivation d → idg (zsubst d a t) = idg d

                              idg is invariant under the eigensubst. idg (zsubst d a t) = idg d for ZDerivation d, substituting a genuine closed term t (IsUTerm, needed only for the zInd rank irk p invariance irk_fvSubst; on the headline path t is a numeral).

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iotil_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ t) (a d : V) :
                              ZDerivation d → iotil (zsubst d a t) = iotil d

                              iotil (pre-ordinal õ) is invariant under the eigensubst. Needs IsUTerm t for the axiom cases (õ(Ax) = oAtomLk reads the principal formula's irk, invariant under fvSubst of a real term).

                              source
                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iord_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUTerm ℒₒᵣ t) (hZ : ZDerivation d) (a : V) :
                              iord (zsubst d a t) = iord d

                              The eigensubst preserves the ordinal iord (route-B I∀ bridge). With this, rewiring red (zIall) = d0(a/n) keeps the ε₀-descent (iord (zsubst d0 e n) = iord d0, so the banked iord_descent_zIall transfers).

                              zReg — hereditary eigenvariable freshness (Path-X O1 foundation) #

                              zReg d = violation count: 0 iff d is regular, i.e. every zIall/zInd node n in d has maxEigen(premise n) < eigenvar(n) (the eigenvariable strictly exceeds every eigenvariable index used in its premise — Buchholz's freshness side-condition). Built by the exact maxEigen/idg table template, folding the max of a per-node freshness flag (ltFlag) and the premise violations.

                              This is the additive O1 architecture (lap 93): rather than baking freshness into zIallWff (which would shrink the ZDerivation fixpoint and force the embedding to re-prove it), zReg is a standalone 𝚺₁ function threaded alongside ZDerivation. The two facts O1 needs — the route-B bound maxEigen d0 < a (from ZRegular (zIall …)) and stability under red — both follow from zReg's recursion equations and zReg_zsubst (regularity is preserved by closed-term substitution, since both maxEigen and the eigenvariables are).

                              source
                              noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ltFlag {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (x y : V) :
                              V

                              ltFlag x y = 0 iff x < y, else 1 — the per-node freshness violation indicator.

                                                  zReg c = zRegNext c (zRegTable (c - 1))

                                                  source

                                                            ZRegular and the route-B freshness bridge #

                                                            source
                                                            def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                            Prop

                                                            Regularity: d is hereditarily eigenvariable-fresh (zReg d = 0).

                                                            Equations
                                                            • GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular d = (GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zReg d = 0)
                                                            Instances For
                                                              source
                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_lt_of_regular_zIall {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 : V} (h : ZRegular (zIall s a p d0)) :
                                                              maxEigen d0 < a

                                                              Route-B bridge (I∀): a regular zIall node has the freshness bound maxEigen d0 < a that the reformulated ZDerivation_zsubst consumes.

                                                              source
                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.maxEigen_lt_of_regular_zInd {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s at' p d0 d1 : V} (h : ZRegular (zInd s at' p d0 d1)) :
                                                              maxEigen d1 < π₁ at'

                                                              Route-B bridge (Ind step premise): a regular zInd node has maxEigen d1 < π₁ at'.

                                                              zReg_zsubst — regularity is preserved by closed-term substitution #

                                                              Since zsubst preserves both maxEigen (maxEigen_zsubst) and the eigenvariables themselves (zsubst_zIall/zInd keep the binder), every per-node freshness flag is unchanged, so zReg is invariant. This is the substitution step of "red preserves regularity" (O1).

                                                              source
                                                              @[simp]
                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zReg_zAxBot {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s : V) :
                                                              zReg (zAxBot s) = 0

                                                              zReg for the tag-8 ⊥-left leaf zAxBot (= 0; the dispatcher defaults unknown tags to 0).

                                                              source
                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zReg_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t d : V) :
                                                              ZDerivation d → zReg (zsubst d a t) = zReg d

                                                              zFresh — hereditary I∀ EIGENVARIABLE-CONDITION freshness (lap 126, soundness-inversion input) #

                                                              zFresh d = violation count: 0 iff every zIall s a p d0 node of d satisfies the I∀ eigenvariable condition — ^&a occurs free in neither the matrix p nor the conclusion antecedent seqAnt s, witnessed by the 𝚫₁, zsubst-stable equations fvSubst a (numeral 0) p = p and fvSubstSeq a (numeral 0) (seqAnt s) = seqAnt s (substituting an a-free closed term fixes a code iff ^&a ∉ FV). Built by the EXACT zReg table template — a standalone 𝚺₁ function threaded alongside ZDerivation (lap-93 additive O1), NOT baked into zIallWff/ZPhi (which would shrink the fixpoint + force the embedding to re-prove it, and break ZDerivation_zsubst). ZFresh d := zFresh d = 0 is the invariant ZDerivesEmptyR carries to supply ZDerivation_iRcritG_critReductCorr's hpfresh/hΓfresh (via fvSubst_numeral_transfer to the cut instance k). NB: unlike zReg (which tracks maxEigen < eigenvar at I∀ AND Ind), zFresh puts a flag only at I∀ (tag 1) — the only node the ∀-inversion inverts; tags 2/3/4 just fold the premises.

                                                              source
                                                              noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqWffFlag {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (Γ : V) :
                                                              V

                                                              seqWffFlag Γ = 0 iff every entry of Γ is a UFormula, else 1 — the antecedent well-formedness indicator that freshFlag/zFresh carry so the I∀-node freshness is preserved by closed substitution (freshFlag_zsubst_eq_zero needs ∀ i, IsUFormula (znth Γ i), which ZDerivation does NOT supply at atom/zAx1 leaves — so it must be tracked as an invariant).

                                                              Equations

                                                                  𝚺₁-Function₁ seqWffFlag via LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.seqWffFlagDef

                                                                  𝚺₁-Function₁ seqWffFlag
                                                                  source
                                                                  @[simp]
                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqWffFlag_eq_zero_iff {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {Γ : V} :
                                                                  seqWffFlag Γ = 0 ↔ ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh Γ, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth Γ i)
                                                                  source
                                                                  noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqAntSeqFlag {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s : V) :
                                                                  V

                                                                  seqAntSeqFlag s = 0 iff the antecedent seqAnt s is a Seq, else 1 — the antecedent Seq-ness indicator, the Seq analogue of seqWffFlag. ZDerivation does NOT supply Seq (seqAnt s) at atom/axiom leaves (seqAnt q := π₁ q is not structurally a Seq, InternalZ:967), so the per-node bundles hAll/hNeg of the ⊥-orbit critical-reduct soundness (Crux2Blueprint, ZDerivation_iRKcCrit_*) — which require Seq (seqAnt sⱼ)/Seq (seqAnt sᵢ) of the chain redex premise nodes — must carry it as a tracked invariant (lap 131). This is the per-node flag the eventual derivation-fold (mirror ZFresh/seqWffFlag) maxes over.

                                                                  Equations

                                                                      theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqAntSeqFlag_eq_zero_iff {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s : V} :
                                                                      seqAntSeqFlag s = 0 ↔ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt s)
                                                                      source
                                                                      theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqWffFlag_fvSubstSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a n Γ : V} (h : seqWffFlag Γ = 0) :
                                                                      seqWffFlag (fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) Γ) = 0

                                                                      Antecedent well-formedness survives fvSubstSeq by a closed numeral (IsUFormula.fvSubst).

                                                                      source
                                                                      noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.eqFlag {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (x y : V) :
                                                                      V

                                                                      eqFlag x y = 0 iff x = y, else 1 — a 𝚺₀ equality-violation indicator (lets the fvSubst-equality freshness conditions be a max-fold of eqFlag (fvSubst …) ·, mirroring ltFlag).

                                                                          noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.freshFlag {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a p Γ : V) :
                                                                          V

                                                                          Per-I∀-node freshness flag: 0 iff ^&a is free in neither the matrix p nor the antecedent Γ, AND every entry of Γ is a UFormula (the seqWffFlag Γ conjunct — needed so the freshness is preserved by closed substitution, since ZDerivation does not supply antecedent wff at leaves).

                                                                              fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) Γ = Γ
                                                                              source
                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.freshFlag_wff {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a p Γ : V} (h : freshFlag a p Γ = 0) (i : V) :
                                                                              i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh Γ → LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth Γ i)

                                                                              Antecedent well-formedness extracted from a vanishing freshFlag.

                                                                              source
                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.freshFlag_eq_zero {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a p Γ : V} (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) p = p) (hΓ : fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) Γ = Γ) (hwff : seqWffFlag Γ = 0) :
                                                                              freshFlag a p Γ = 0

                                                                              freshFlag = 0 constructor from the two non-occurrence equalities + antecedent well-formedness.

                                                                              source
                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.freshFlag_zsubst_eq_zero {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {a e n p Γ : V} (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ p) (h : freshFlag e p Γ = 0) :
                                                                              freshFlag e (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) p) (fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n) Γ) = 0

                                                                              Per-I∀-node freshFlag is preserved (downward) by closed-numeral substitution — the I∀ step of zFresh_zsubst. The eigenvariable e is unchanged by zsubst d a (numeral n); if e is fresh in the matrix p and antecedent Γ of the node, it stays fresh in fvSubst a (numeral n) p / fvSubstSeq a (numeral n) Γ (substituting a different/closed numeral cannot introduce ^&e). The antecedent well-formedness needed for the structural commutation is now carried IN freshFlag itself (seqWffFlag), so only the matrix IsUFormula p (free from zIallWff) is an external hypothesis.

                                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zFresh_eq_zFreshNext {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {c : V} (hpos : 0 < c) :
                                                                                              zFresh c = zFreshNext c (zFreshTable (c - 1))

                                                                                              zFresh recursion equations + per-node extraction #

                                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqMaxAux_eq_zero_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s ds : V} (j : V) :
                                                                                              (∀ i < j, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth s (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = 0) → iseqMaxAux s ds j = 0

                                                                                              The iseqMaxAux max-fold of a value table is 0 when every read value is 0.

                                                                                              source
                                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zFresh_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s r ds : V) :
                                                                                              zFresh (zK s r ds) = iseqMaxTab (zFreshTable (zK s r ds - 1)) ds

                                                                                              zFresh over a chain (tag-4 folds the premises, no own flag): zFresh (zK s r ds) is the max-fold of the premises' zFresh read out of the recursion table.

                                                                                              source
                                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zfresh_zK_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : zFresh (zK s r ds) = 0) {i : V} (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                              zFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = 0

                                                                                              A premise of a fresh chain node is itself fresh.

                                                                                              source
                                                                                              theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zfresh_zK_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, zFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = 0) :
                                                                                              zFresh (zK s r ds) = 0

                                                                                              A chain node all of whose premises are fresh is fresh.

                                                                                              source

                                                                                              Prop

                                                                                              Hereditary I∀ eigenvariable-condition freshness.

                                                                                              Equations

                                                                                              Instances For
                                                                                                source
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.freshFlag_eq_zero_of_zfresh_zIall {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 : V} (h : ZFresh (zIall s a p d0)) :
                                                                                                freshFlag a p (seqAnt s) = 0

                                                                                                The per-I∀-node freshness flag of a fresh derivation vanishes.

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zfresh_zIallPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 : V} (h : ZFresh (zIall s a p d0)) :
                                                                                                ZFresh d0

                                                                                                A fresh derivation's I∀ premise is itself fresh.

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubst_numeral_eq_self_of_zfresh_zIall {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 : V} (h : ZFresh (zIall s a p d0)) :
                                                                                                LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) p = p

                                                                                                Per-node extraction (matrix). From ZFresh (zIall s a p d0), the eigenvariable a is fresh in the matrix p: fvSubst a (numeral 0) p = p. Fed through fvSubst_numeral_transfer to supply hpfresh at any cut instance k.

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubstSeq_numeral_eq_self_of_zfresh_zIall {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 : V} (h : ZFresh (zIall s a p d0)) :
                                                                                                fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) (seqAnt s) = seqAnt s

                                                                                                Per-node extraction (antecedent). The hΓfresh analogue: fvSubstSeq a (numeral 0) (seqAnt s) = seqAnt s.

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubst_numeral_eq_self_of_zfresh_zIall_at {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (k : V) {s a p d0 : V} (h : ZFresh (zIall s a p d0)) (hp : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ p) :
                                                                                                LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral k) p = p

                                                                                                Target-3 supplier hpfresh (matrix, ANY instance k). From the orbit-carried ZFresh (zIall s a p d0) plus the matrix well-formedness IsUFormula p, the eigenvariable a is fresh in p at any closed numeral instance k: fvSubst a (numeral k) p = p. This is exactly the hpfresh hypothesis of ZDerivation_iRcritG_critReductCorr at the L-redex instance k = π₁(π₂(tp …)); the numeral 0 witness stored in freshFlag is lifted to k by fvSubst_numeral_transfer. The matrix IsUFormula p is supplied at the call site by the I∀ node's own ZDerivation wff data (hwff.2.2.isUFormula).

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fvSubstSeq_numeral_eq_self_of_zfresh_zIall_at {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (k : V) {s a p d0 : V} (h : ZFresh (zIall s a p d0)) :
                                                                                                fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral k) (seqAnt s) = seqAnt s

                                                                                                Target-3 supplier hΓfresh (antecedent, ANY instance k). The antecedent analogue, supplying ZDerivation_iRcritG_critReductCorr's hΓfresh at the L-redex instance k. NO external hypothesis is needed: the antecedent well-formedness ∀ i, IsUFormula (znth (seqAnt s) i) that fvSubstSeq_numeral_transfer requires comes FREE from the folded seqWffFlag (freshFlag_wff), so the orbit-carried ZFresh alone discharges it.

                                                                                                zFresh_zsubst — freshness is preserved DOWNWARD by closed-numeral substitution #

                                                                                                Unlike zReg_zsubst (an equality), zFresh only preserves downward: at an I∀ node whose eigenvariable is the substituted a, zsubst removes ^&a everywhere, which can only lower the violation count. So the invariant is the implication ZFresh d → ZFresh (zsubst d a (numeral n)). The I∀ step is freshFlag_zsubst_eq_zero (with the antecedent well-formedness now carried inside freshFlag); the chain step folds the premises via zfresh_zK_of/zfresh_zK_premise; the other rules fold premises directly. This is the substitution step of "red preserves ZFresh" (the freshness analogue of ZRegular_red).

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                @[simp]
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zFresh_zAxBot {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s : V) :
                                                                                                zFresh (zAxBot s) = 0

                                                                                                zFresh for the tag-8 ⊥-left leaf zAxBot (= 0; the dispatcher defaults unknown tags to 0).

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zFresh_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a n d : V) :
                                                                                                ZDerivation d → ZFresh d → ZFresh (zsubst d a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral n))

                                                                                                zSeqAnt — hereditary antecedent Seq-ness (lap 133, the shared Seq(seqAnt) blocker) #

                                                                                                The per-node bundles hAll/hNeg of the ⊥-orbit critical-reduct soundness (Crux2Blueprint, ZDerivation_iRKcCrit_*) require Seq (seqAnt sⱼ) / Seq (seqAnt sᵢ) of the chain redex premise nodes. ZDerivation does NOT supply Seq (seqAnt s) at atom/axiom leaves (ZPhi records only inAnt/membership, InternalZ:5362; seqAnt q := π₁ q is not structurally a Seq, InternalZ:967). So it must be carried as a tracked invariant — the Seq analogue of zFresh, threaded alongside ZDerivation (lap-93 additive O1), NOT baked into ZPhi (which would shrink the fixpoint + force the embedding/ZDerivation_zsubst to re-prove it, the lap-130/131 finding). Unlike zFresh (a flag only at I∀), zSeqAnt puts seqAntSeqFlag (fstIdx d) at EVERY node (we need Seq (seqAnt …) of arbitrary chain premises), then folds the premises by the EXACT zFreshNext template. The chain (tag-4) node carries NO own flag (exactly like zFresh_zK) — so ZSeqAnt_red's reduct cases mirror ZFresh_red line-for-line; the redex premises we need Seq (seqAnt …) for (I∀/I¬/axAll/axNeg, tags 1/2/5/6) all DO flag. ZSeqAnt d := zSeqAnt d = 0 is the invariant ZDerivesEmptyR will carry to supply the per-node Seq (seqAnt sⱼ)/Seq (seqAnt sᵢ) (seq_seqAnt_zK_premise).

                                                                                                source
                                                                                                noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAntNext {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d s : V) :
                                                                                                V

                                                                                                Per-node antecedent-Seq fold step: max the node's own seqAntSeqFlag (fstIdx d) (now at EVERY node INCLUDING chain nodes zTag d = 4 — the lap-152 tag-4 fold, so a ZSeqAnt-clean chain supplies Seq (seqAnt s) of its OWN end-sequent, needed by genReduct_chain_hasRedex's principal-cut soundness) with the premise fold (the EXACT zFreshNext premise template).

                                                                                                          noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                          V

                                                                                                          Violation count zSeqAnt d: 0 iff every node of d has a Seq antecedent.

                                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt_eq_zSeqAntNext {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {c : V} (hpos : 0 < c) :
                                                                                                                zSeqAnt c = zSeqAntNext c (zSeqAntTable (c - 1))

                                                                                                                zSeqAnt recursion equations + per-node extraction. Each node carries its own #

                                                                                                                seqAntSeqFlag (fstIdx ·) head term (unlike zFresh, where only I∀ flags).

                                                                                                                zSeqAnt (zAtom s) = seqAntSeqFlag s

                                                                                                                zSeqAnt (zAxBot s) = seqAntSeqFlag s

                                                                                                                zSeqAnt for the tag-8 ⊥-left leaf zAxBot — mirror zAtom (dispatchers default unknown tags to 0). (zReg_zAxBot/zFresh_zAxBot are hoisted earlier, before their zsubst-soundness uses.)

                                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (s r ds : V) :
                                                                                                                zSeqAnt (zK s r ds) = seqAntSeqFlag s ⊔ iseqMaxTab (zSeqAntTable (zK s r ds - 1)) ds

                                                                                                                zSeqAnt over a chain (lap-152 tag-4 fold: now ALSO carries the own head flag seqAntSeqFlag s, maxed with the premise fold).

                                                                                                                source
                                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt_zK_premise_zero {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : zSeqAnt (zK s r ds) = 0) {i : V} (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                zSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = 0

                                                                                                                A premise of a zSeqAnt-clean chain node is itself zSeqAnt-clean.

                                                                                                                source
                                                                                                                def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                                Prop

                                                                                                                Hereditary antecedent-Seq-ness.

                                                                                                                Equations
                                                                                                                • GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt d = (GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt d = 0)
                                                                                                                Instances For
                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seq_seqAnt_fstIdx_of_zSeqAnt {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hpos : 0 < d) (h : zSeqAnt d = 0) :
                                                                                                                  LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d))

                                                                                                                  Own-node antecedent-Seq extraction (UNGUARDED, lap-152 fold). Every ZSeqAnt-clean positive node — INCLUDING chain (tag-4) nodes — now carries its own head flag seqAntSeqFlag (fstIdx d), so it has a Seq antecedent. This is the lap-152 fold's payoff: genReduct_chain_hasRedex gets Seq (seqAnt s) of the chain's own end-sequent.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seq_seqAnt_of_zSeqAnt {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hpos : 0 < d) (_hnK : zTag d ≠ 4) (h : zSeqAnt d = 0) :
                                                                                                                  LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d))

                                                                                                                  A ZSeqAnt-clean node has a Seq antecedent (compat wrapper of seq_seqAnt_fstIdx_of_zSeqAnt; the zTag d ≠ 4 guard is now vacuous after the lap-152 fold but kept so callers need not change).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seq_seqAnt_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (h : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) :
                                                                                                                  LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt s)

                                                                                                                  The chain's OWN end-sequent is a Seq from its ZSeqAnt — the lap-152 tag-4-fold supplier that unblocks genReduct_chain_hasRedex's principal-cut soundness.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqAntSeqFlag_zK_of_ZSeqAnt {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (h : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) :
                                                                                                                  seqAntSeqFlag s = 0

                                                                                                                  The chain's own head flag vanishes from its ZSeqAnt (flag form of seq_seqAnt_zK).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.Seq_seqAnt_tpReduce {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {I s n : V} (hs : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt s)) :
                                                                                                                  LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (tpReduce I s n))

                                                                                                                  tpReduce preserves antecedent-Seq-ness (every branch: Rep keeps s; R-∀/L-¬ seqSetSucc keep seqAnt s; R-¬/L-∀ seqAddAnt cons onto it). Needed for the lap-152 head-flag of the red-reduct's conclusion (red_zK_rep/_nonchain reduce the conclusion via tpReduce).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zDerivation_pos {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hd : ZDerivation d) :
                                                                                                                  0 < d

                                                                                                                  Every ZDerivation code is positive (each constructor is ⟪…⟫ + 1).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seq_seqAnt_zK_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds i : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hpos : ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) (hnK : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) ≠ 4) :
                                                                                                                  LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)))

                                                                                                                  The per-node Seq (seqAnt sⱼ) supplier that hAll/hNeg consume: a chain premise of a ZSeqAnt-clean K-node (with the premise a genuine ZDerivation) has a Seq antecedent.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt_zK_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hs : seqAntSeqFlag s = 0) (h : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, zSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = 0) :
                                                                                                                  zSeqAnt (zK s r ds) = 0

                                                                                                                  A chain node all of whose premises are zSeqAnt-clean is zSeqAnt-clean (the K-node carries no own flag). Constructor analogue of zfresh_zK_of; used to push ZSeqAnt through reducts.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  @[simp]
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqAntSeqFlag_fvSubstSeqt {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t s : V) :
                                                                                                                  seqAntSeqFlag (fvSubstSeqt a t s) = 0

                                                                                                                  The conclusion antecedent of any zsubst node is a fvSubstSeq image, hence always a Seq, so its head flag vanishes — seqAntSeqFlag (fvSubstSeqt a t s) = 0.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt_zsubst {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (a t d : V) :
                                                                                                                  ZDerivation d → zSeqAnt (zsubst d a t) = 0

                                                                                                                  zsubst always yields antecedent-Seq derivations (UNCONDITIONAL). Every node of zsubst d a t concludes fvSubstSeqt a t (·), whose antecedent is a fvSubstSeq image (always a Seq, seqAntSeqFlag_fvSubstSeqt), so ZSeqAnt (zsubst d a t) needs NO ZSeqAnt d hypothesis (contrast zFresh_zsubst, only downward). This is the I∀-reduct substitution step of "red preserves ZSeqAnt" (red_zIall = zsubst d0 a 0).

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZSeqAnt — structural + Ind cases (lap 133, mirror ZFresh_red_of_not_zK) #

                                                                                                                  The structural rules strip to a premise (red_zIneg = d0, hereditary) or substitute (red_zIall = zsubst d0 a 0, antecedent-Seq UNCONDITIONALLY by zSeqAnt_zsubst); the Ind reduct is a chain over ⟨d1,…,d0⟩ (premises hereditary + the Ind node's own conclusion Seq (seqAnt s) from its head flag); atoms/axioms are red-identities. The zK chain dispatch (the reduct-conclusion Seq obligations) is the remaining frontier.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zSeqAnt_iIndReductSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d0 d1 k : V} (h0 : zSeqAnt d0 = 0) (h1 : zSeqAnt d1 = 0) (i : V) :
                                                                                                                  i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k) → zSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k) i) = 0

                                                                                                                  Every premise of the Ind reduct sequence ⟨d1,…,d1,d0⟩ is zSeqAnt-clean when d0,d1 are.

                                                                                                                  source
