                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_red_of_not_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hZ : ZDerivation d) (hsa : ZSeqAnt d) (hnK : zTag d ≠ 4) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (red d)

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZSeqAnt (structural + Ind cases). The chain (zK) case is the remaining frontier (the reduct-conclusion Seq obligations from the K-node own flag).

                                                                                                                  Regularity of the corrected-reduct premises (engine re-key prerequisite, lap 119) #

                                                                                                                  The re-keyed tag-4 critical reduct iRKcCrit (InternalZ) replaces each redex premise by its genuine §3.2-case-5.1 reduct: the I∀ R-redex by zsubst (zIallPrem dᵢ) (zIallEig dᵢ) (numeral k) (re-principalized child), the I¬ R-redex by zInegPrem dᵢ (the I¬ child), and the L-redex axioms by zAx1 nodes. For the ZRegular_red (O1) front of the engine swap, these new premises must be shown regular. The zAx1 slots are free (zReg_zAx1 = 0); the two below are the genuine facts.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zsubst_zIallPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {e k : V} (he : ZDerivation e) (hreg : ZRegular e) (htag : zTag e = 1) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (zsubst (zIallPrem e) (zIallEig e) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral k))

                                                                                                                  The ∀ R-redex's corrected-reduct premise is regular. The §3.2-case-5.1 reduct re-principalizes the I∀ child d0 at a closed numeral; zReg_zsubst (closed-term substitution preserves regularity) plus the I∀ node's own regularity (zReg d0 = 0) gives that the substituted premise is regular.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zInegPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {e : V} (he : ZDerivation e) (hreg : ZRegular e) (htag : zTag e = 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (zInegPrem e)

                                                                                                                  The ¬ R-redex's corrected-reduct premise is regular. The §3.2-case-5.1 ¬-reduct is the I¬ child d0 = zInegPrem e; regularity is hereditary (zReg_zIneg : zReg (zIneg ..) = zReg d0).

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZRegular — the structural and Ind cases (Path-X O1, lap 93) #

                                                                                                                  red is the genuine one-step reduction. For regularity preservation ZRegular d → ZRegular (red d): the structural rules strip to a premise (red_zIall = d0, red_zIneg = d0) or are the identity (atoms/axioms), so regularity is immediate; the Ind reduct iRInd = zK s (irk p) (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 1) is a chain over the literal premises ⟨d1, d0⟩ (no substitution at this level), so its zReg is max (zReg d1) (zReg d0). The remaining case is the chain dispatch red (zK …) = iRK … (5.1/5.2.1/5.2.2), the genuinely hard step (it threads zReg_zsubst through the critical reduct's splice/replace).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqRegAux_znth_congr {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds ds' : V} (j : V) :
                                                                                                                  (∀ i < j, LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds' i) → iseqRegAux ds j = iseqRegAux ds' j

                                                                                                                  zReg-fold congruence on agreeing entries (znth form, mirror iseqMaxIdgAux_congr).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqReg_seqCons {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds v : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) :
                                                                                                                  iseqReg (ds ⁀' v) = iseqReg ds ⊔ zReg v

                                                                                                                  zReg-fold over a seqCons: iseqReg (seqCons ds v) = max (iseqReg ds) (zReg v).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqRegAux_const {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds c : V} (hconst : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, zReg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = c) (j : V) :
                                                                                                                  0 < j → j ≤ LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds → iseqRegAux ds j = c

                                                                                                                  zReg-fold over a constant block: if every entry's zReg is c, the fold is c (for 0<j).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqReg_iRepeatSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {v k : V} (hk : 0 < k) :
                                                                                                                  iseqReg (iRepeatSeq v k) = zReg v

                                                                                                                  zReg-fold of a constant block iRepeatSeq v k: = zReg v (for 0<k).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqReg_iIndReductSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d0 d1 k : V} (hk : 0 < k) :
                                                                                                                  iseqReg (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k) = zReg d1 ⊔ zReg d0

                                                                                                                  zReg-fold of the Ind reduct sequence: max (zReg d1) (zReg d0) (for 0<k).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_red_of_not_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hZ : ZDerivation d) (hreg : ZRegular d) (hnK : zTag d ≠ 4) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (red d)

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZRegular (structural + Ind cases). Stated per the ZDerivation constructor; the chain (zK) case is the remaining frontier (the iRK dispatch).

                                                                                                                  Reusable building blocks for the zK chain case (5.1/5.2.1/5.2.2) #

                                                                                                                  All three iRK branches produce a chain whose premises are regular reducts. These are the shared lemmas: a chain with all-regular premises is regular (ZRegular_zK_of_premises), and the per-premise atomic reduct zAxReduct preserves regularity. The remaining zK work is to show each branch's premise sequence (seqUpdate/iCritReductSeq/splice) has all-regular entries — then these close it.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iseqReg_eq_zero_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds : V} (h : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, zReg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = 0) :
                                                                                                                  iseqReg ds = 0

                                                                                                                  A chain iseqReg-fold vanishes when every premise is regular.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zK_of_premises {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (zK s r ds)

                                                                                                                  A K-chain all of whose premises are regular is regular. The shared closing lemma for the three iRK branches (each reduct is a chain over regular premises).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zAxReduct {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {x : V} (h : ZRegular x) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (zAxReduct x)

                                                                                                                  zAxReduct preserves regularity. On atomic axioms it returns a zAx1 node (zReg = 0); otherwise it is the identity. So a regular premise yields a regular per-premise reduct.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.le_iseqRegAux {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds : V} (j i : V) :
                                                                                                                  i < j → zReg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) ≤ iseqRegAux ds j

                                                                                                                  Every premise's zReg is dominated by the chain fold (mirror le_iseqMaxEigen).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.le_iseqReg {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {ds i : V} (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                  zReg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) ≤ iseqReg ds
                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zK_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds i : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : ZRegular (zK s r ds)) (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)

                                                                                                                  A premise of a regular chain is regular (the converse of ZRegular_zK_of_premises; needed to extract the splice halves' regularity in the 5.2.1 case).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zK_of_seqUpdate {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' ds i v : V} (hall : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) (hv : ZRegular v) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (zK s' r' (seqUpdate ds i v))

                                                                                                                  Regularity of a seqUpdate chain (5.2.2 replace iRKr, and each half of 5.1 iRKc): replacing one premise by a regular reduct keeps the chain regular.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zK_of_iCritReductSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' d0 d1 : V} (h0 : ZRegular d0) (h1 : ZRegular d1) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (zK s' r' (iCritReductSeq d0 d1))

                                                                                                                  Regularity of an iCritReductSeq chain (5.1 critical iRcritG/iRKc): the two-element chain ⟨d0, d1⟩ is regular when both halves are.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_iRKcCrit {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hprem : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq d), ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) m)) (hdI : ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d))) (hregI : ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d))) (htagI : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d)) = 1 ∨ zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d)) = 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (iRKcCrit d)

                                                                                                                  The re-keyed critical reduct iRKcCrit is regular. The engine swap's ZRegular_red_zK_crit re-proof target: each of the two iCritReductSeq halves is a seqUpdate of the chain's premise sequence swapping one redex premise for its §3.2-case-5.1 corrected reduct. Regular when (a) every original premise is (hprem, from the chain's own regularity) and (b) the swapped reduct is — the I∀ slot via ZRegular_zsubst_zIallPrem, the I¬ slot via ZRegular_zInegPrem, the L-redex zAx1 slots free (zReg_zAx1). The R-redex must be an I-rule (htagI: tag 1 or 2), which holds on the orbit (tp dᵢ = isymR). Polarity-dispatch matches iRKcCrit's own zTag dᵢ = 1 branch.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_iRKcCrit_of_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hreg : ZRegular (zK s r ds)) (hvalid : zKValid s r ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (iRKcCrit (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  The corrected reduct of a valid critical chain is regular — front-1 of the engine swap, CLOSED additively. Discharges every hypothesis of ZRegular_iRKcCrit directly from a valid critical chain zK s r ds: premise regularity from the chain's own ZRegular (ZRegular_zK_premise), the R-redex premise's ZDerivation from chain inversion (zDerivation_zK_inv), and htagI (R-redex is an I-rule) from the redex-pair certificate (zTag_redexI_of_zKValid). Once the engine swaps red (zK s r ds) ↦ iRKcCrit (zK s r ds), ZRegular_red_zK_crit is rw [red_zK_crit hcrit]; exact this — the O1 front of the swap is now pure wiring.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_zK_of_seqInsert {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' ds i a b : V} (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hall : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) (ha : ZRegular a) (hb : ZRegular b) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (zK s' r' (seqInsert ds i a b))

                                                                                                                  Regularity of a seqInsert chain (5.2.1 splice iRKs): inserting two regular halves a,b in place of premise i keeps the chain regular. The 5.2.1 analogue of ZRegular_zK_of_seqUpdate, via the pointwise read-out forall_znth_seqInsert.

                                                                                                                  red-preserves-ZRegular, the zK chain dispatch (5.1 / 5.2.1 / 5.2.2) #

                                                                                                                  red (zK s r ds) dispatches via iRK on two criticality sentinels (red_zK_crit/_rep/_splice). Each branch reduct is a chain over a seqUpdate/seqInsert/iCritReductSeq of ds with one or two premises swapped for already-tabulated reducts red dᵢ. The structural-block lemmas above close the seqUpdate/iCritReductSeq branches standalone from the IH (ZRegular (red premise)); the seqInsert (5.2.1) branch additionally needs the two splice halves znth (zKseq (red dᵢ)) {0,1} regular, which holds when red dᵢ is a chain (tag 4) — exactly the zKValidF-supplied fact threaded inside redSound (lap-93 finding). So _replace/_crit are unconditional; _splice takes the halves' regularity as an explicit hypothesis.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.red_zK_rep {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) :
                                                                                                                  red (zK s r ds) = zK (tpReduce (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)))) s 0) r (seqUpdate ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)) (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)))))

                                                                                                                  5.2.2 replace recursion equation (port of the Crux2Blueprint red_zK_rep, here in the build): non-critical chain whose least-permissible premise is itself non-critical ⟹ red swaps premise i = permIdx d for its tabulated reduct red dᵢ.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.red_zK_splice {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : ¬permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) = 4) :
                                                                                                                  red (zK s r ds) = zK s (irk (seqSucc (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 0))) ⊔ r) (seqInsert ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 0) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 1))

                                                                                                                  5.2.1 splice recursion equation (lap-95 GATED dispatch): non-critical chain d whose least-permissible premise dᵢ is itself a CRITICAL CHAIN (zTag dᵢ = 4 AND dᵢ critical) ⟹ red splices dᵢ's two reduct-halves znth (zKseq (red dᵢ)) {0,1} in place at i. The zTag dᵢ = 4 gate (htag) is the lap-95 faithfulness fix: only a genuine chain has meaningful reduct-halves.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.red_zK_rep_nonchain {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) ≠ 4) :
                                                                                                                  red (zK s r ds) = zK (tpReduce (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)))) s 0) r (seqUpdate ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)) (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)))))

                                                                                                                  5.2.2 replace recursion equation for a NON-CHAIN selected premise (lap-95 GATED dispatch). When the least-permissible premise dᵢ is not a chain (zTag dᵢ ≠ 4) — atom / I-rule / axiom — the gated iRK routes it to the replace branch iRKr (Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5.2.2) regardless of dᵢ's permIdx sentinel. This is the lap-94 obstruction's cure: the OLD iRK mis-spliced such premises (permIdx dᵢ = 0 = lh(zKseq dᵢ) triggered the splice by default); the gate now sends them to replace.

                                                                                                                  I∀ conclusion-tracking — red (zIall …) derives the tpReduce'd sequent (route-B, lap 98) #

                                                                                                                  The replace branch of ZDerivation_red_zK (Crux2Blueprint.lean:206/214) needs, for a NON-Rep selected premise dᵢ, that the I∀ reduct red dᵢ = zsubst d0 a 0 carries exactly the reduced end-sequent tpReduce (R_∀xF) (end dᵢ) 0 = Γ→F(0) — the lap-97 eigensubst made red dᵢ derive Γ→F(0); this lemma certifies its end-sequent IS Γ→F(0), so the conclusion-reduced chain validity (isChainInf on the swapped premise) can consume it. The I∀ analogue of the proved I¬ red_zIneg_tpReduce (InternalZ.lean:7521); harder because I∀ substitutes the eigenvariable, so it needs the eigenvariable-freshness facts a ∉ FV(p) / a ∉ FV(Γ) (Buchholz's eigenvariable condition, O3 — supplied on the orbit by the embedding's fresh-eigenvariable choice).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.red_zIall_tpReduce {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 : V} (hZ : ZDerivation (zIall s a p d0)) (hpfresh : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) p = p) (hΓfresh : fvSubstSeq a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral 0) (seqAnt s) = seqAnt s) :
                                                                                                                  LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (red (zIall s a p d0)) = tpReduce (tp (zIall s a p d0)) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (zIall s a p d0)) 0

                                                                                                                  I∀ reduct end-sequent = the tpReduce'd sequent. Given the eigenvariable a is fresh in the matrix p (hpfresh) and in the conclusion antecedent Γ = seqAnt s (hΓfresh), the I∀ reduct red (zIall s a p d0) = zsubst d0 a 0 has end-sequent tpReduce (tp (zIall …)) s 0 = Γ→F(0). This is the route-B conclusion-tracking fact for the ∀-principal cut (red_zIneg_tpReduce is its I¬ sibling).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqSucc_zsubst_zIall_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsSemiterm ℒₒᵣ 0 t) (hZ : ZDerivation (zIall s a p d0)) (hpfresh : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p = p) :
                                                                                                                  seqSucc (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (zsubst d0 a t)) = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.substs1 ℒₒᵣ t p

                                                                                                                  The ∀-inversion building block (general instance t). Substituting the I∀ eigenvariable a by ANY closed term t in the premise derivation d0 yields a derivation whose succedent is the INSTANCE F(t) = substs1 t p. Generalizes red_zIall_tpReduce (the t = 0 case that red currently fixes).

                                                                                                                  ⚠️ This is the lap-114 crux finding. The critical-cut SOUNDNESS inversion ZDerivation_red_zK_crit (Crux2Blueprint:100) reduces, via ZDerivation_iRcritG_of, to two stripped half-derivations haux0 (Γ → cutFormula d) / haux1. The left half's threading (isChainInf) forces its R-redex premise to derive exactly cutFormula d = F(k), where k is the L-redex instance (cutFormula_all). But the reduct red supplies there is zAxReduct (red premise) = zsubst d0 a (numeral 0) — instance 0, NOT k. So haux0 is UNPROVABLE for ρ = zAxReduct ∘ red: red's critical reduct is unsound (it substitutes the wrong instance). Instance-0 is correct for the ordinal DESCENT (iord (zsubst d0 a n) is instance- invariant) but wrong for SOUNDNESS, which needs Buchholz §3.2 case 5.1 re-principalization at k. The fix: the critical reduct's R-redex premise must be zsubst d0 a (numeral k). This lemma is its succedent identity — with k the L-redex instance, zsubst d0 a (numeral k) derives Γ → F(k) = Γ → cutFormula d, so haux0's threading closes. The reduct is a ZDerivation by ZDerivation_zsubst_zIall_premise; the matrix/Γ freshness hpfresh (a ∉ FV p, the eigenvariable condition, Buchholz O3) is supplied on the ⊥-orbit by the embedding's fresh-eigenvariable choice. See PENDING_WORK lap-114.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.seqSucc_corrected_redexI_eq_cutFormula {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d sᵢ a p d0 : V} (hIall : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d) = zIall sᵢ a p d0) (hpremZ : ZDerivation (zIall sᵢ a p d0)) (hpfresh : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral (π₁ π₂ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexJ d)))) p = p) :
                                                                                                                  seqSucc (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (zsubst d0 a (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral (π₁ π₂ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexJ d)))))) = cutFormula d

                                                                                                                  The corrected critical reduct's R-redex premise derives cutFormula d (second linchpin). When the redexI premise of a critical chain d is an I∀ node zIall sᵢ a p d0 (R-principal for ∀p), the re-principalized reduct zsubst d0 a (numeral k) at the L-redex instance k = π₁(π₂(tp (redexJ premise))) — the SAME k that cutFormula reads — derives succedent exactly cutFormula d. Combines seqSucc_zsubst_zIall_premise (the instance-k succedent) with cutFormula_all (cutFormula d = F(k) in the ∀-branch). This is what makes the corrected haux0's isChainInf j₀=redexI succedent clause hold — the step red's instance-0 reduct cannot provide (lap-114 finding). Modulo the eigenvariable freshness hpfresh (Buchholz O3, supplied on the ⊥-orbit).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.fstIdx_zsubst_zIall_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 t : V} (ht : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsSemiterm ℒₒᵣ 0 t) (hZ : ZDerivation (zIall s a p d0)) (hpfresh : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.fvSubst ℒₒᵣ a t p = p) (hΓfresh : fvSubstSeq a t (seqAnt s) = seqAnt s) :
                                                                                                                  LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (zsubst d0 a t) = seqSetSucc s (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.substs1 ℒₒᵣ t p)

                                                                                                                  The corrected reduct's FULL end-sequent (general instance t). Generalizes red_zIall_tpReduce from the fixed t = 0 to any closed term: the eigensubst premise zsubst d0 a t of a valid I∀ node has end-sequent Γ → F(t) = seqSetSucc s (substs1 t p) (antecedent Γ kept by hΓfresh, succedent the instance F(t)). The antecedent half is what haux0's hant/hX_ant need; the succedent half is linchpin #1.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_red_zK_replace {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hreg : ZRegular (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZRegular (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.2 replace branch — regularity preserved (unconditional). red (zK s r ds) = K^r(i/red dᵢ); regular since every original premise is (ZRegular_zK_premise) and the swapped reduct red dᵢ is (IH).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_red_zK_crit {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hreg : ZRegular (zK s r ds)) (hI : ZRegular (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds))))) (hJ : ZRegular (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds))))) (hcrit : ¬permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.1 critical branch — regularity preserved. red (zK s r ds) = iRcritG … is a chain over iCritReductSeq d{0} d{1}, each half a seqUpdate of ds swapping a redex premise for its tabulated reduct red (znth ds (redexI/J)); regular when those two reducts are (supplied — they are IH instances once the redex indices are in range).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_iRcritG_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d ρk : V} {ρ : V → V} (h : ZRegular (iRcritG d ρ)) (hk : ρk < 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (iRcritG d ρ)) ρk)

                                                                                                                  Premise extraction from a critical reduct iRcritG d ρ. Its premise sequence is the two-element iCritReductSeq, so when the whole reduct is regular both halves znth (zKseq (iRcritG d ρ)) {0,1} are. The extraction the 5.2.1 splice needs for the halves of red dᵢ once dᵢ is known to be a chain.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_red_zK_splice {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hreg : ZRegular (zK s r ds)) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : ¬permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) = 4) (ha : ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 0)) (hb : ZRegular (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 1)) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.1 splice branch — regularity preserved, given the halves are regular. red (zK s r ds) splices the two halves a,b = znth (zKseq (red dᵢ)) {0,1} in place at i; regular when every original premise is (ZRegular_zK_premise) and a,b are. The halves' regularity holds when red dᵢ is a chain (tag 4), discharged inside redSound from the zKValidF-supplied tag (lap-93 finding) — here an explicit hypothesis.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_red_zK_splice_of_chain {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hreg : ZRegular (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZRegular (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : ¬permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) (hchain : ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)))) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) = 4) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.1 splice branch — regularity preserved, from the selected premise being a CHAIN. Strengthens ZRegular_red_zK_splice: the two splice halves' regularity is derived from zTag dᵢ = 4 (the selected premise dᵢ is itself a chain) together with the IH ZRegular (red dᵢ). Since the splice branch is taken exactly when dᵢ is critical (h2), red dᵢ = iRcritG dᵢ … is a two-premise critical reduct, so its halves are premises of a regular chain (ZRegular_iRcritG_premise). This is the interface redSound consumes — the zTag dᵢ = 4 fact comes from the zKValidF validity data threaded through the induction (lap-93 finding).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_red_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hreg : ZRegular (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZRegular (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) :
                                                                                                                  ZRegular (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZRegular — the full zK chain case (lap-95: hseltag DISCHARGED). Dispatches on the GATED iRK (lap 95): permIdx (zK s r ds) < lh ds (chain non-critical) splits on whether the selected premise dᵢ is a chain (zTag dᵢ = 4); a chain dispatches further on dᵢ's own criticality (non-critical → replace ZRegular_red_zK_replace, critical → splice ZRegular_red_zK_splice_of_chain with zTag dᵢ = 4 now supplied by the gate), while a NON-chain goes to the conclusion-replace red_zK_rep_nonchain (the lap-94 obstruction's cure — the OLD iRK mis-spliced non-chains). The 5.1 critical branch's redex bounds are discharged INTERNALLY from the chain's own validity (zKValidF_of_ZDerivation_zK + zKCritical_of_not_permIdx_lt + redexI_redexJ_lt_of_zKValid). The former leaf hseltag is GONE: under the gated dispatch the splice branch IS the zTag dᵢ = 4 case. This is the regularity (O1) half of "red preserves valid+regular", now UNCONDITIONAL; the validity half needs the tpReduce conclusion-reduction (lap-90).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZRegular_red {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                                  ZDerivation d → ZRegular d → ZRegular (red d)

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZRegular — the full structural theorem (O1, UNCONDITIONAL). The eigenvariable freshness (Buchholz's side-condition, tracked by zReg) is hereditarily preserved by the genuine reduct red. Assembled by zDerivation_induction: every non-chain node delegates to ZRegular_red_of_not_zK (structural / Ind / axiom cases, with the I-rule eigenvar-strip kept fresh by zReg_zIall/zReg_zIneg); the chain (zK) node delegates to the unconditional ZRegular_red_zK, feeding the per-premise IH ZRegular (red dᵢ) (each premise regular by ZRegular_zK_premise). This is the O1 half of "red preserves valid + regular" — the validity half (ZDerivation_red_zK, Crux2Blueprint) is the remaining frontier.

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZFresh — the structural and Ind cases (freshness analogue of ZRegular_red) #

                                                                                                                  ZFresh is the eigenvariable-condition invariant the LEFT-branch ∀-soundness consumes. Like ZRegular, it is preserved by the genuine reduct red, but only downward (an implication, since red_zIall substitutes the eigenvariable away — zFresh_zsubst). The structural rules strip to a premise (red_zIall = zsubst d0 a 0, red_zIneg = d0), the Ind reduct is a chain over ⟨d1,…,d0⟩, and atoms/axioms are identities.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zfresh_iIndReductSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d0 d1 k : V} (h0 : zFresh d0 = 0) (h1 : zFresh d1 = 0) (i : V) :
                                                                                                                  i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k) → zFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k) i) = 0

                                                                                                                  Every premise of the Ind reduct sequence ⟨d1,…,d1,d0⟩ is fresh when d0,d1 are.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_red_of_not_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hZ : ZDerivation d) (hfresh : ZFresh d) (hnK : zTag d ≠ 4) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (red d)

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZFresh (structural + Ind cases). The chain (zK) case is the remaining frontier (mirrors ZRegular_red_zK's replace/splice/crit dispatch).

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZFresh — the zK chain dispatch (freshness analogue of ZRegular_red_zK) #

                                                                                                                  Mirrors the ZRegular_red_zK_* family. Since zFresh (zK s r ds) is purely the premise max-fold (the chain node carries NO own freshness flag — zFresh_zK), each branch reduces to "every reduct premise is ZFresh", closed by zfresh_zK_of. The replace/crit/splice reduct shapes are the same seqUpdate/iCritReductSeq/seqInsert of ds as for regularity, so the structural-block lemmas below are line-for-line analogues of ZRegular_zK_of_seqUpdate/_iCritReductSeq/_seqInsert.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zAxReduct {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {x : V} (h : ZFresh x) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (zAxReduct x)

                                                                                                                  zAxReduct preserves ZFresh. On atomic axioms it returns a zAx1 node (zFresh = 0); otherwise it is the identity.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zK_of_premises {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (zK s r ds)

                                                                                                                  A chain all of whose premises are fresh is fresh (ZFresh wrapper of zfresh_zK_of).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zK_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds i : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : ZFresh (zK s r ds)) (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)

                                                                                                                  A premise of a fresh chain is fresh (ZFresh wrapper of zfresh_zK_premise).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zK_of_seqUpdate {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' ds i v : V} (hall : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) (hv : ZFresh v) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (zK s' r' (seqUpdate ds i v))

                                                                                                                  ZFresh of a seqUpdate chain (5.2.2 replace / each half of 5.1 critical).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zK_of_iCritReductSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' d0 d1 : V} (h0 : ZFresh d0) (h1 : ZFresh d1) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (zK s' r' (iCritReductSeq d0 d1))

                                                                                                                  ZFresh of an iCritReductSeq chain (5.1 critical iRcritG).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zK_of_seqInsert {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' ds i a b : V} (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hall : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) (ha : ZFresh a) (hb : ZFresh b) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (zK s' r' (seqInsert ds i a b))

                                                                                                                  ZFresh of a seqInsert chain (5.2.1 splice).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_iRcritG_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d ρk : V} {ρ : V → V} (h : ZFresh (iRcritG d ρ)) (hk : ρk < 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (iRcritG d ρ)) ρk)

                                                                                                                  Premise extraction from a critical reduct iRcritG d ρ (freshness analogue of ZRegular_iRcritG_premise).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_red_zK_replace {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hfresh : ZFresh (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZFresh (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.2 replace branch — ZFresh preserved (unconditional).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_red_zK_crit {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hfresh : ZFresh (zK s r ds)) (hI : ZFresh (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds))))) (hJ : ZFresh (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds))))) (hcrit : ¬permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.1 critical branch — ZFresh preserved.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_red_zK_splice {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hfresh : ZFresh (zK s r ds)) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : ¬permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) = 4) (ha : ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 0)) (hb : ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 1)) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.1 splice branch — ZFresh preserved, given the halves are fresh.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_red_zK_splice_of_chain {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hfresh : ZFresh (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZFresh (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : ¬permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) (hchain : ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)))) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) = 4) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.1 splice branch — ZFresh preserved, from the selected premise being a CHAIN. Mirror of ZRegular_red_zK_splice_of_chain: the splice halves' freshness is derived from zTag dᵢ = 4 + the IH ZFresh (red dᵢ) (red dᵢ = iRcritG dᵢ … is a two-premise critical reduct, halves are premises of a fresh chain via ZFresh_iRcritG_premise).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_red_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hfresh : ZFresh (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZFresh (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZFresh — the full zK chain case. Same gated iRK dispatch as ZRegular_red_zK: chain-selected non-critical → replace; chain-selected critical → splice; non-chain → conclusion-replace; fully-critical → critical reduct (redex bounds from the chain's own validity).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_red {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                                  ZDerivation d → ZFresh d → ZFresh (red d)

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZFresh — the full structural theorem (downward). The I∀ eigenvariable-condition freshness invariant is preserved by the genuine reduct red. Assembled by zDerivation_induction: every non-chain node delegates to ZFresh_red_of_not_zK; the chain (zK) node delegates to ZFresh_red_zK, feeding the per-premise IH ZFresh (red dᵢ) (each premise fresh by ZFresh_zK_premise).

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZSeqAnt — the zK chain dispatch (lap 133, mirror ZFresh_red_zK) #

                                                                                                                  Since the chain node carries NO own zSeqAnt flag (zSeqAnt_zK, exactly like zFresh_zK), each reduct branch reduces to "every reduct premise is ZSeqAnt-clean", closed by zSeqAnt_zK_of — a line-for-line analogue of the ZFresh_red_zK_* family. The one genuine delta from zFresh: ZSeqAnt_zAxReduct must extract Seq (seqAnt (fstIdx x)) from the axiom premise's own head flag (vs zFresh_zAx1 = 0 free).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_zAxReduct {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {x : V} (h : ZSeqAnt x) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (zAxReduct x)

                                                                                                                  zAxReduct preserves ZSeqAnt. On L-axioms (tags 5/6) it returns a zAx1 whose conclusion is the axiom's own fstIdx x, so its head flag vanishes from the axiom's own ZSeqAnt (seq_seqAnt_of_zSeqAnt, the axiom is non-chain); otherwise zAxReduct is the identity.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_zK_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds i : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (h : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)

                                                                                                                  A premise of a ZSeqAnt-clean chain is ZSeqAnt-clean (ZSeqAnt wrapper of zSeqAnt_zK_premise_zero).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_zK_of_seqUpdate {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' ds i v : V} (hs : seqAntSeqFlag s' = 0) (hall : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) (hv : ZSeqAnt v) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (zK s' r' (seqUpdate ds i v))

                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt of a seqUpdate chain (5.2.2 replace / each half of 5.1 critical).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_zK_of_iCritReductSeq {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' d0 d1 : V} (hs : seqAntSeqFlag s' = 0) (h0 : ZSeqAnt d0) (h1 : ZSeqAnt d1) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (zK s' r' (iCritReductSeq d0 d1))

                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt of an iCritReductSeq chain (5.1 critical iRcritG).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_zK_of_seqInsert {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s' r' ds i a b : V} (hs : seqAntSeqFlag s' = 0) (hi : i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hall : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) (ha : ZSeqAnt a) (hb : ZSeqAnt b) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (zK s' r' (seqInsert ds i a b))

                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt of a seqInsert chain (5.2.1 splice).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_iRcritG_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d ρk : V} {ρ : V → V} (h : ZSeqAnt (iRcritG d ρ)) (hk : ρk < 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (iRcritG d ρ)) ρk)

                                                                                                                  Premise extraction from a critical reduct iRcritG d ρ (ZSeqAnt analogue of ZFresh_iRcritG_premise).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_zsubst_zIallPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {e k : V} (he : ZDerivation e) (htag : zTag e = 1) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (zsubst (zIallPrem e) (zIallEig e) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral k))

                                                                                                                  The I∀ R-redex's corrected-reduct premise is ZSeqAnt-clean (UNCONDITIONAL). The §3.2-case-5.1 ∀-reduct substitutes the I∀ child zIallPrem e = d0 by numeral k; zsubst is antecedent-Seq for free (zSeqAnt_zsubst, no ZSeqAnt e hypothesis), needing only ZDerivation e (⟹ ZDerivation d0). ZSeqAnt analogue of ZRegular_zsubst_zIallPrem.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_zInegPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {e : V} (he : ZDerivation e) (hsa : ZSeqAnt e) (htag : zTag e = 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (zInegPrem e)

                                                                                                                  The I¬ R-redex's corrected-reduct premise zInegPrem e = d0 inherits ZSeqAnt from the I¬ node (zSeqAnt_zIneg decomposes as a max, so the premise flag is dominated). ZSeqAnt analogue of ZRegular_zInegPrem.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_iRKcCrit {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hprem : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq d), ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) m)) (hdI : ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d))) (hsaI : ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d))) (hSeqD : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d))) (hSeqJ : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexJ d))))) (htagI : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d)) = 1 ∨ zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d)) = 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (iRKcCrit d)

                                                                                                                  The re-keyed critical reduct of a valid critical chain is ZSeqAnt-clean — O-ZSeqAnt front of the engine swap (red-FREE). Mirrors ZFresh_iRKcCrit/ZRegular_iRKcCrit: both halves of the iCritReductG are seqUpdate chains whose premises are clean (hprem), one slot a zsubst/zInegPrem redex premise (ZSeqAnt_zsubst_zIallPrem/ZSeqAnt_zInegPrem), the other the §5 Ax^1 whose antecedent stays a Seq (seqAddAnt/seqSetSucc of the cut-partner's Seq antecedent hSeqJ).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_iRKcCrit_of_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hsa : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (hvalid : zKValid s r ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (iRKcCrit (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (iRKcCrit (zK s r ds)) from a valid critical chain — discharges every ZSeqAnt_iRKcCrit hypothesis from the orbit data (parallel to ZRegular_iRKcCrit_of_zK). The cut-partner's Seq antecedent hSeqJ comes from seq_seqAnt_zK_premise (the partner is an L-axiom, tp = isymLk ⟹ zTag ∈ {5,6} ≠ 4, tp_isymLk_tag).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_red_zK_replace {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hsa : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZSeqAnt (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.2 replace branch — ZSeqAnt preserved (unconditional).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_red_zK_crit {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hsa : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (hI : ZSeqAnt (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds))))) (hJ : ZSeqAnt (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds))))) (hcrit : ¬permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.1 critical branch — ZSeqAnt preserved.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_red_zK_splice {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hsa : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : ¬permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) = 4) (ha : ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 0)) (hb : ZSeqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) 1)) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.1 splice branch — ZSeqAnt preserved, given the halves are clean.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_red_zK_splice_of_chain {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hsa : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZSeqAnt (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (h1 : permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (h2 : ¬permIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))))) (hchain : ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds)))) (htag : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (permIdx (zK s r ds))) = 4) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  5.2.1 splice branch — ZSeqAnt preserved, from the selected premise being a CHAIN.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_red_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hsa : ZSeqAnt (zK s r ds)) (hred : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, ZSeqAnt (red (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) :
                                                                                                                  ZSeqAnt (red (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZSeqAnt — the full zK chain case. Same gated iRK dispatch as ZFresh_red_zK.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZSeqAnt_red {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                                  ZDerivation d → ZSeqAnt d → ZSeqAnt (red d)

                                                                                                                  red preserves ZSeqAnt — the full structural theorem. Antecedent-Seq-ness is preserved by the genuine reduct red. Assembled by zDerivation_induction: non-chain nodes delegate to ZSeqAnt_red_of_not_zK; the chain node to ZSeqAnt_red_zK, feeding the per-premise IH.

                                                                                                                  ZFresh_iRKcCrit — the re-keyed critical reduct preserves freshness (O3 front of the engine swap) #

                                                                                                                  The O3 (freshness) analogue of the landed ZRegular_iRKcCrit (O1). The engine swap re-keys red's tag-4 critical branch to emit iRKcCrit d (the re-principalized corrected reduct); this proves that reduct preserves ZFresh, additively, before the atomic swap. The reduct's two iCritReductSeq halves are seqUpdates of the chain's premise sequence swapping one redex premise for its §3.2-case-5.1 corrected reduct — the I∀ slot via ZFresh_zsubst_zIallPrem (zFresh_zsubst on the I∀ child), the I¬ slot via ZFresh_zInegPrem (hereditary zFresh_zIneg), the L-redex zAx1 slots free (zFresh_zAx1).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zsubst_zIallPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {e k : V} (he : ZDerivation e) (hfresh : ZFresh e) (htag : zTag e = 1) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (zsubst (zIallPrem e) (zIallEig e) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.Arithmetic.numeral k))

                                                                                                                  The ∀ R-redex's corrected-reduct premise is fresh (O3 analogue of ZRegular_zsubst_zIallPrem). The §3.2-case-5.1 ∀-reduct is zsubst d0 aᵢ (numeral k) (re-principalized at the L-instance k); its freshness is the downward zFresh_zsubst applied to the I∀ child d0 = zIallPrem e.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_zInegPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {e : V} (he : ZDerivation e) (hfresh : ZFresh e) (htag : zTag e = 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (zInegPrem e)

                                                                                                                  The ¬ R-redex's corrected-reduct premise is fresh (O3 analogue of ZRegular_zInegPrem). The I¬ child d0 = zInegPrem e is fresh hereditarily (zFresh_zIneg).

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_iRKcCrit {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (hprem : ∀ m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq d), ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) m)) (hdI : ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d))) (hfreshI : ZFresh (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d))) (htagI : zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d)) = 1 ∨ zTag (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (zKseq d) (redexI d)) = 2) :
                                                                                                                  ZFresh (iRKcCrit d)

                                                                                                                  The re-keyed critical reduct iRKcCrit preserves ZFresh — O3 front of the engine swap, CLOSED additively (mirror of ZRegular_iRKcCrit). Each of the two iCritReductSeq halves is a seqUpdate of the chain's premise sequence swapping one redex premise for its corrected reduct; fresh when (a) every original premise is (hprem, from the chain's own ZFresh) and (b) the swapped reduct is — the I∀ slot via ZFresh_zsubst_zIallPrem, the I¬ slot via ZFresh_zInegPrem, the L-redex zAx1 slots free.

                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZFresh_iRKcCrit_of_zK {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hfresh : ZFresh (zK s r ds)) (hvalid : zKValid s r ds) :
